purpose: Build a shared admin-repair feature foundation for Windows 11 and Ubuntu diagnosis and bounded repair flows, without enforcing full KRITIS hardening in this phase.
goal: Ananta can guide admins through bounded diagnosis, repair planning, controlled execution and verification with explicit evidence and future-ready hardening hooks.

two_line_strategy.fast_product_line: Deliver useful shared admin-repair capabilities quickly (diagnosis, plans, dry-run, guided execution, verification).
two_line_strategy.kritis_line: Later harden proven capabilities with Audit, MutationGate, ApprovalPolicy, deterministic workflows, sandboxing and RBAC.
two_line_strategy.current_track_position: This admin repair track is shared foundation work; full KRITIS enforcement is intentionally deferred.

non_goals: Do not implement full KRITIS hardening in this track.
Do not create a separate repair-specific security architecture.
Do not bypass hub-governed policy boundaries for risky mutations.

kritis_hardening_hooks.hook_policy: Fields are prepared and surfaced now; strict KRITIS gates are connected later.

milestones.title: Shared repair foundation
milestones.target_outcome: Mode definition, entrypoint, environment summary and bounded evidence model are stable.

milestones.title: Diagnosis and planning
milestones.target_outcome: Problem taxonomy, diagnosis artifact and bounded repair plan generation are operational.

milestones.title: Controlled execution
milestones.target_outcome: Dry-run, step-confirmed execution and verification flow are available.

milestones.title: Platform adapters and playbooks
milestones.target_outcome: Windows/Ubuntu evidence adapters and first repair playbooks are usable.

milestones.title: Hook-ready quality baseline
milestones.target_outcome: Compatibility hooks, audit-ready outputs and smoke/contract tests are in place.

tasks.title: Use-case and shared mode definition
acceptance_criteria: Admin repair mode is documented as shared feature foundation for product line and future KRITIS line.
Windows 11 and Ubuntu diagnosis scope is explicit.
Review-first behavior is default and autonomous unrestricted shell behavior is excluded.

tasks.title: CLI entrypoint and mode options
acceptance_criteria: A discoverable CLI entrypoint exists for diagnose/repair mode.
Dry-run and bounded execution options are available at entry level.
Help text explains shared foundation scope and deferred KRITIS hardening.

tasks.title: OS detection and environment summary
acceptance_criteria: CLI detects Windows 11 vs Ubuntu reliably for supported paths.
Environment summary includes shell/runtime/package/service basics.
Summary is shown before planning mutations.

tasks.title: Bounded evidence collection contract
acceptance_criteria: Allowed evidence sources and bounded collection rules are explicit.
Sensitive/irrelevant data is redacted or excluded by default.
Evidence list is visible and reviewable before repair actions.

tasks.title: Problem taxonomy for admin repair
acceptance_criteria: Problem classes cover package/runtime, PATH, permissions, services, ports and container/runtime setup issues.
Taxonomy supports diagnosis, routing and repair planning.
Taxonomy wording is platform-neutral with OS-specific examples.

tasks.title: Diagnosis artifact model
acceptance_criteria: Diagnosis artifact includes likely causes, evidence links, confidence and next steps.
Artifact is readable for operators and reusable in follow-up planning.
Artifact structure is stable enough for later contract testing.

tasks.title: Repair action schema hooks prepare
acceptance_criteria: Repair actions carry hook-ready fields: risk_class, requires_approval, dry_run_supported, verification_required, mutation_candidate.
Repair actions also carry evidence_sources, execution_scope, audit_hint, repair_action_class, affected_targets, expected_verification.
Fields are informational in this phase and do not force full KRITIS gating yet.

tasks.title: Bounded repair plan generation
acceptance_criteria: Diagnosis can be transformed into ordered bounded repair actions.
Each action includes expected effect and verification hint.
Plan distinguishes low-risk suggestions from higher-risk mutation candidates.

tasks.title: Repair action classification policy
acceptance_criteria: Actions are classified into allow, confirm-required and blocked.
Classification maps to risk_class, requires_approval and execution_scope fields.
Policy is compatible with future ApprovalPolicy/MutationGate integration.

tasks.title: Dry-run by default for repair plans
acceptance_criteria: Repair plans can be previewed without mutation.
Planned commands/actions are explicit and inspectable.
dry_run_supported is surfaced per action.

tasks.title: Step-confirmed execution mode
acceptance_criteria: Actions can be executed one step at a time with explicit confirmation.
Accepted, skipped and blocked actions are recorded.
Execution can be stopped safely between steps.

tasks.title: Post-repair verification phase
acceptance_criteria: Verification checks run after executed actions.
Result states clearly: resolved, improved, unchanged or regressed.
verification_required and expected_verification are reflected in results.

tasks.title: Rollback and caution model
acceptance_criteria: Reversible vs non-reversible actions are explicit in plan output.
High-risk mutation_candidate actions show stronger caution language.
Rollback limitations are visible before confirmation.

tasks.title: Future hardening bridge contracts
acceptance_criteria: Repair action and verification outputs expose stable identifiers for later Audit and MutationGate linkage.
Approval-ready metadata is emitted without enforcing full KRITIS workflow now.
No second orchestration loop is introduced; hub remains control plane.

tasks.title: Windows evidence adapters
acceptance_criteria: Windows evidence adapters collect bounded event/service/runtime context.
Collection errors degrade cleanly and remain visible.
Evidence is normalized into artifacts and evidence_sources.

tasks.title: Ubuntu evidence adapters
acceptance_criteria: Ubuntu evidence adapters collect bounded journal/service/package context.
Collection errors degrade cleanly and remain visible.
Evidence is normalized into artifacts and evidence_sources.

tasks.title: Windows repair playbooks
acceptance_criteria: Initial Windows playbooks cover runtime/path, service and container/WSL-like issues.
Playbooks produce bounded action plans with affected_targets.
Playbooks stay compatible with dry-run and confirmation flow.

tasks.title: Ubuntu repair playbooks
acceptance_criteria: Initial Ubuntu playbooks cover apt/dpkg, service and runtime/toolchain issues.
Playbooks produce bounded action plans with affected_targets.
Playbooks stay compatible with dry-run and confirmation flow.

groups.title: Operator UX observability docs and quality

tasks.title: Admin-facing output structure
acceptance_criteria: CLI output separates diagnosis, plan, risk and verification clearly.
Raw log dumps are contextualized with concise interpretation.
Output remains readable in terminal-first workflows.

tasks.title: Audit-ready repair session trail
acceptance_criteria: Session records include evidence, proposed actions, confirmations and verification outcomes.
audit_hint is emitted per action/session for later KRITIS audit integration.
Sensitive material handling remains explicit.

tasks.title: Golden path Windows
acceptance_criteria: One reproducible Windows flow exists from evidence to verification.
Flow documents where confirmation gates occur.
Flow demonstrates shared foundation behavior without full KRITIS enforcement.

tasks.title: Golden path Ubuntu
acceptance_criteria: One reproducible Ubuntu flow exists from evidence to verification.
Flow documents where confirmation gates occur.
Flow demonstrates shared foundation behavior without full KRITIS enforcement.

tasks.title: Contract and smoke tests
acceptance_criteria: Diagnosis/plan/action/verification output contracts are tested.
At least one bounded smoke scenario exists for Windows and Ubuntu.
Tests verify confirmation gating and dry-run behavior.

tasks.title: Future extension boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Future domains (network/container/app-specific repair) stay explicitly outside MVP scope.
Expansion path is documented without inflating current track complexity.
Shared foundation stays maintainable and reviewable.

execution_order_summary: Start with ARC-FOUNDATION to stabilize shared mode, evidence bounds and diagnosis model.
Continue with ARC-REPAIR-FLOW to deliver dry-run, confirmation-gated execution and verification.
Build ARC-PLATFORM-ADAPTERS for Windows and Ubuntu evidence/playbook coverage.
Finish ARC-UX-DOCS-QUALITY with operator output, audit-ready traces, docs and tests.
Keep KRITIS hardening as next-line integration on top of produced hooks, not in this track.
